model_assumption: splicing dynamics can be approximated by deterministic ordinary differential equations that imply continuous cell-state transition vectors
distance_metric: velocity vector cosine similarity; phase portrait likelihood
optimization_objective: fit phase portraits and maximize latent-time consistency with observed spliced/unspliced density
input_object: AnnData with spliced and unspliced layers
output_object: velocity vectors; latent time; transition graph
transferable_mechanism: Dynamical state-transition modeling may inspire perturbation or trajectory direction hypotheses when splicing layers exist.
known_limitations: Strongly depends on kNN smoothing and kinetic assumptions; heterogeneous systems or complex splicing can cause distorted or reversed trajectories.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: mathematical assumptions and caveats validated; keep as RNA-velocity-specific mechanism, not a generic trajectory solver.

model_assumption: gene expression evolves smoothly along one-dimensional pseudotime or lineage curves
distance_metric: negative binomial likelihood with spline basis functions
optimization_objective: optimize spline regression weights and run Wald tests over lineages
input_object: count matrix plus pseudotime and lineage weights
output_object: trajectory-associated genes; Wald test statistics; pattern clusters
transferable_mechanism: Smooth lineage-wise regression can transfer to ordered perturbation, dose, or time-response settings with explicit continuous covariates.
known_limitations: Does not infer trajectories; requires high-confidence external pseudotime and lineage weights.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: accepted only when ordered covariates or lineage weights exist.

model_assumption: cell populations evolve between time snapshots through transport-like couplings with growth/death regularization
distance_metric: regularized Earth mover / Kantorovich optimal transport distance
optimization_objective: infer probabilistic couplings and transport matrices between time points
input_object: time-resolved expression snapshots
transferable_mechanism: Population transport formulation can inspire temporal mapping for sparse developmental or perturbation time courses.
known_limitations: Requires dense developmental time-series sampling; fails for jump-like or long-interval perturbation transitions.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: migration use must require a shared state space and dense time points.

model_assumption: cross-time, cross-space, or cross-modality states can be approximated by matching heterogeneous cost structures
distance_metric: fused Gromov-Wasserstein transport cost
optimization_objective: optimize couplings while balancing intra-modality and inter-modality costs
input_object: AnnData objects with features, time, spatial, or batch covariates
output_object: transport plans; mappings; lineage or spatial correspondences
transferable_mechanism: Flexible cost design can transfer to spatial, temporal, or cross-modality mapping problems where direct tools are immature.
known_limitations: Computational cost can scale quadratically with cells and depends heavily on user-defined cost matrices.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: accepted as high-skill exploratory mechanism requiring manual fused-cost tuning.

model_assumption: discrete perturbation effects can be decomposed into additive linear transcriptomic shifts
distance_metric: linear model residual fit; OLS or ridge regression
optimization_objective: estimate perturbation coefficients by minimizing residual likelihood
input_object: Perturb-seq expression matrix plus perturbation labels
transferable_mechanism: Additive perturbation-effect modeling can seed conservative treatment/control response hypotheses.
known_limitations: Captures only additive linear shifts; misses nonlinear epistasis and multimodal response landscapes.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: native perturbation mechanism, but must carry nonlinear-response caveat.

model_assumption: cell-state transitions can be modeled as stochastic random walks over local neighborhood graphs
distance_metric: transition probabilities; Schur decomposition
optimization_objective: coarse-grain transition matrix into macrostates and estimate absorption probabilities
input_object: AnnData with transition matrix, velocity, pseudotime, or kernels
transferable_mechanism: Kernel composition and fate absorption ideas can transfer to novel dynamic-state analyses.
known_limitations: Framework quality is bounded by the input transition kernel; not a DE or perturbation model by itself.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: validated as topology/fate mapping mechanism with kernel-quality dependency.

model_assumption: spatial spots are linear mixtures of fine-grained reference cell-type expression profiles
distance_metric: negative binomial likelihood; Bayesian posterior inference
optimization_objective: infer absolute cell-type abundance posterior per spatial location
input_object: spatial count matrix plus matched scRNA-seq reference
transferable_mechanism: Bayesian count deconvolution can inspire migration to other mixture-resolution spatial assays.
known_limitations: Requires matched high-quality scRNA-seq reference; not a self-supervised spatial representation model.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: dedicated deconvolution mechanism; do not conflate with spatial foundation models.

model_assumption: heterogeneous multi-omics layers share sparse, interpretable latent factors with view-specific structure
distance_metric: Bayesian multi-view factor likelihood; variance explained
optimization_objective: maximize multi-view reconstruction likelihood with sparse orthogonal latent factors
input_object: matched multi-omics matrices
output_object: latent factors; feature weights; variance decomposition
transferable_mechanism: Multi-view factorization can transfer to matched multi-omics perturbation or sparse joint representation problems.
known_limitations: Not optimized for all clustering metrics; strongest value is interpretable biology, pathway factors, and survival-associated latent structure.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: scoped to interpretable latent factors, not universal clustering superiority.

model_assumption: discrete count features can be generated from continuous latent variables while accounting for technical covariates
distance_metric: negative binomial or zero-inflated negative binomial likelihood; ELBO
optimization_objective: maximize variational lower bound for batch-aware latent representation
input_object: AnnData count matrix with batch or covariate annotations
output_object: latent embedding; denoised expression; batch-aware representation
transferable_mechanism: Probabilistic latent-variable modeling can transfer to new single-cell representation and uncertainty tasks.
known_limitations: Black-box deep model; small datasets may overfit and require careful training diagnostics.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: benchmark support must remain task-specific.

model_assumption: batch effects can be corrected in low-dimensional embedding space through entropy-regularized soft clustering
distance_metric: cosine distance in embedding space; entropy-regularized cluster cost
optimization_objective: optimize batch-diverse soft clusters and batch-specific correction vectors
input_object: PCA or low-dimensional embedding
transferable_mechanism: Embedding-level correction can transfer to fast integration scenarios with precomputed representations.
known_limitations: Does not model raw counts; correction ceiling is locked by the quality of the input PCA or embedding matrix.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: accepted as embedding-correction mechanism only.

model_assumption: neighborhood graphs capture local cellular manifold structure for standard single-cell workflows
distance_metric: kNN graph distances; PCA and UMAP metrics
optimization_objective: build scalable preprocessing, graph, clustering, and analysis workflows
input_object: AnnData
transferable_mechanism: AnnData-centered graph construction and workflow patterns can transfer to new single-cell pipeline designs.
known_limitations: Toolkit, not a single algorithm; benchmark claims must be task-specific and cannot generalize across all Scanpy functions.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: boundary tightened to workflow/toolkit mechanism, not one canonical algorithm.

model_assumption: shared biological states can be linked by anchors across batches or modalities
distance_metric: CCA/RPCA/WNN neighborhood similarity
optimization_objective: find anchors, weighted nearest neighbors, and integrated representations
input_object: SeuratObject; multimodal single-cell objects
transferable_mechanism: Anchor and WNN ideas can transfer to cross-modal or multi-view matching hypotheses.
known_limitations: Version and workflow specific; claims must specify Seurat version and task, and cannot be treated as a single fixed algorithm.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: I/O contract locked to SeuratObject and multimodal workflow patterns.

model_assumption: self-supervised Transformer attention can capture gene-expression context and cell representations
distance_metric: attention similarity; embedding distance; masked expression reconstruction loss
optimization_objective: pretrain gene/cell representations with masked modeling and contrastive objectives
input_object: gene expression matrix or tokenized cell representation
output_object: cell embeddings; gene embeddings; prediction outputs
transferable_mechanism: Foundation embeddings can seed exploratory transfer for representation, annotation, or perturbation-response hypotheses.
known_limitations: Third-party downstream biological evaluation is still early; embeddings cannot directly establish causal regulatory networks.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: feature extraction only for migration; block direct causal GRN claims.

model_assumption: pretrained cellular representations may transfer across single-cell tasks
distance_metric: embedding similarity; attention patterns
optimization_objective: pretrain or fine-tune cellular representation models
input_object: expression matrix
transferable_mechanism: Pretrained cell embeddings may transfer to rare tasks where direct supervised tools are absent.
known_limitations: Independent third-party benchmarks are limited; migration claims must remain exploratory.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: accepted as foundation representation profile with benchmark caveat.

model_assumption: cell representations are modulated by local spatial microenvironment or niche context
distance_metric: embedding similarity; transformer attention over expression and context
optimization_objective: learn context-aware cellular embeddings with autoregressive or contrastive objectives
input_object: single-cell or spatial expression with context features
transferable_mechanism: Niche-aware embeddings may inspire exploratory microenvironment or spatial-context modeling.
known_limitations: Must never replace dedicated spatial deconvolution tools such as cell2location without task-specific evidence.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: boundary tightened to exploratory niche representation, not deconvolution.

model_assumption: doublets resemble artificial merged profiles in expression neighborhood space
distance_metric: pANN; nearest-neighbor proportion
optimization_objective: classify cells by artificial doublet neighborhood enrichment
input_object: SeuratObject or expression matrix
transferable_mechanism: Artificial example generation can transfer to anomaly detection tasks with synthetic controls.
known_limitations: Tuned for scRNA-seq doublets; not a generic contamination or artifact detector without a new simulator.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: I/O contract and synthetic-positive mechanism validated.

model_assumption: observed doublets are close to simulated transcriptomic doublets in kNN space
distance_metric: kNN similarity to simulated doublets
optimization_objective: score cells by simulated-doublet neighborhood enrichment
input_object: count matrix
transferable_mechanism: Simulation-based anomaly scoring can inspire exploratory QC migration for sparse artifact classes.
known_limitations: Requires task-specific synthetic positive generator and threshold validation; not designed for ambient RNA removal.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: accepted as transferable simulation/anomaly mechanism with simulator caveat.

model_assumption: empty-droplet or background profiles approximate additive ambient contamination
distance_metric: marker and background contamination estimates
optimization_objective: estimate and subtract ambient RNA fraction from droplet counts
input_object: droplet count matrix with clusters and background profile
transferable_mechanism: Background-profile subtraction can transfer to spatial or non-droplet contamination-removal hypotheses.
known_limitations: Requires meaningful background profile and marker preservation checks; spatial use needs background-spot reinterpretation.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: accepted as contamination-modeling profile, not generic QC.

model_assumption: reference-trained classifiers generalize to query datasets with similar cell-type coverage
distance_metric: logistic regression confidence or probability scores
optimization_objective: assign cell types from curated reference models
input_object: expression matrix
transferable_mechanism: Supervised reference-label transfer can seed annotation hypotheses for related tissues.
known_limitations: Performance depends on reference coverage, label granularity, and domain shift.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: I/O contract locked to expression matrix and curated reference models.

model_assumption: cell labels can be assigned by correlation to reference expression profiles
distance_metric: Spearman or correlation similarity
optimization_objective: match query cells to reference cell-type profiles
input_object: expression matrix plus reference profiles
transferable_mechanism: Reference correlation scoring can transfer to conservative annotation tasks with strong reference atlases.
known_limitations: Reference quality and label hierarchy dominate performance; weak for unseen cell states.
reviewer_notes: Human reviewed: I/O and reference-dependence validated.
